---
title: "Candidate Notes"
page-layout: custom
---

```{=html}
<!-- Embedded Notes Viewer -->
<div id="notes-container"></div>

<script>
// Fetch and inject the notes viewer
fetch('notes.html')
  .then(response => response.text())
  .then(html => {
    const parser = new DOMParser();
    const doc = parser.parseFromString(html, 'text/html');
    const container = doc.querySelector('.container');
    
    // Remove back link since we have Quarto nav
    const backLink = container.querySelector('.back-link');
    if (backLink) {
      backLink.remove();
    }
    
    document.getElementById('notes-container').appendChild(container);
    
    // Load styles
    const link = document.createElement('link');
    link.rel = 'stylesheet';
    link.href = 'styles.css';
    document.head.appendChild(link);
    
    // Load notes script
    const script = document.createElement('script');
    script.src = 'notes.html'; // Will execute inline script
    document.body.appendChild(script);
  });
</script>

<style>
#notes-container {
  max-width: 1200px;
  margin: 0 auto;
  padding: 20px;
}
</style>
```

## About Notes

Your notes are **persistently stored** in your browser's localStorage. Each note includes:

### Captured Information

**Candidate Details:**
- Full name
- Current title & company
- Email address
- Location
- Years of experience
- Complete skills list

**Note Details:**
- Your written comments
- Rating (1-5 stars)
- Creation timestamp
- Last updated timestamp

### Export Fields (CSV)

When you export notes, you get these columns:

```
Candidate ID | First Name | Last Name | Email | Current Title | 
Company | Location | Years Experience | Skills | Rating | Note | Created At
```

---

## Managing Notes

### View All Notes

All notes are displayed on this page, sorted by newest first. Each note card shows:
- Candidate profile summary
- Full note text
- Rating (⭐ stars)
- Timestamp
- Skills tags

### Export Options

**CSV Export**: Opens in Excel, Google Sheets, or any spreadsheet app
- Best for: Reporting, sharing, analysis

**JSON Export**: Structured data format
- Best for: Developers, data processing, backup

### Clear Notes

Use **"🗑️ Clear All Notes"** to delete everything (with confirmation).

::: {.callout-warning}
**Warning**: Clearing notes is permanent and cannot be undone! Export first if you might need them later.
:::

---

## Statistics Dashboard

The notes page shows live statistics:

| Metric | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Total Notes** | Count of all saved notes |
| **Candidates with Notes** | Unique candidates you've reviewed |
| **Average Rating** | Mean rating across all rated candidates |

---

## Best Practices

✅ **Add notes immediately** after reviewing candidates  
✅ **Rate consistently** (1-5 stars) for easy filtering later  
✅ **Export weekly** as backup before clearing browser cache  
✅ **Include specific details** - skills gaps, salary concerns, culture fit  
✅ **Tag with keywords** for easier searching later  

❌ **Don't rely solely on localStorage** - export regularly  
❌ **Don't clear browser cache** without exporting first  
❌ **Avoid vague notes** - be specific about strengths/concerns  

---

## Data Privacy

- Notes are stored **locally in your browser** (localStorage)
- **Not synced** across devices or browsers
- **Not sent to any server** - completely client-side
- **Not shared** with other users of the system
- **Persist** across sessions on same browser/device

::: {.callout-tip}
**Multi-device users**: Export notes regularly and import them on other devices by copying the localStorage data (advanced users only).
:::

---

## Accessing Notes Programmatically

For developers, notes are stored in localStorage under key:

```javascript
const notes = localStorage.getItem('candidate_matcher_notes');
// Structure: { candidate_id: [{note_text, rating, created_at}, ...] }
```

To export manually:
```javascript
JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('candidate_matcher_notes'))
```
